tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Around bluegill beds, use a 3/8oz Thunder Cricket tipped with a Blade Minnow. 95% of the time green pumpkin. Start on the outer edge of spawning beds — that's where big bass hang out.'

KVD: 'Summer shallow — use a bigger squarebill (2.5 or 4.0) in bluegill patterns around spawning panfish. Both have plenty of vibration for dirty water. Conditions dictate — high dirty water means shallow bass even in summer.'
KVD: 'After power-fishing through an area, clean up with a Texas-rigged Game Hawg and light 1/4oz weight. Lighter weight lets the bait fall slower so the appendages dance through the area.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Conditions over calendar. High, off-colored water sets the stage for shallow bass even in summer. More structure in shallows means more bass. The bluegill spawn is a coast-to-coast pattern — bass target spawning panfish for big meals.'

KVD: 'Bluegills spawn midway back in a pocket, not all the way in the back. They like sides of secondary points and shade trees that stick over water — almost guaranteed under a big willow tree. Use Mega 360 or Side Imaging, or find them with your eyes.'
